title: Bonafousia undulata
taxonomy: Bonafousia undulata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. This plant is native to Madagascar and belongs to the genus Bonafousia. Further classification includes the family Apocynaceae, which is a large family of flowering plants commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. The class of this plant is Magnoliopsida, and it belongs to the kingdom Plantae. The plant's classification also includes the division Magnoliophyta and the phylum Angiospermophyta.

intro: Bonafousia undulata, also known as Wild Cinnamon or Pimenta, is a perennial shrub native to Madagascar, belonging to the family Rutaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its purported health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which are said to aid in wound healing, fever reduction, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Bonafousia undulata include limonoids, alkaloids, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Bonafousia undulata is commonly prepared as an infusion, decoction, or tincture, and is used to treat various ailments, including respiratory infections, fever, and skin conditions.
